description_long: This object is a vintage reel-to-reel audio player, featuring a rectangular body with a combination of light gray and dark gray plastic casing. The top surface displays two prominent circular reels, one for playback and one for recording, with a metallic spindle at their centers. A set of large, tactile control buttons, including a distinctive red button, are positioned on the front panel, alongside a horizontal grille likely for speaker output. The sides of the device show various ports and connectors, including a power socket and audio jacks, while the overall build suggests a robust, portable design. The surface is mostly smooth with some matte and glossy finishes, and the object appears to be constructed primarily from molded plastic with some metal components. This type of device would typically be found in urban environments such as garages, workshops, or retro-themed public spaces.
description: A vintage reel-to-reel audio player with dual reels, large control buttons, and a sturdy plastic and metal build, featuring ports and a speaker grille.
description_view_0: Front view showing the two reels, audio jacks, and part of the control panel.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the reels and the sloped control panel with visible buttons.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the large speaker grille and the main set of control buttons, including a red button.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the reels and additional ports or connectors.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
